commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens WhatsApp QA-lab scenarios, adds evidence posture reporting, updates WhatsApp QA docs, and adds small WhatsApp runtime support for same-chat message actions and group participant identity.

PR surface: Source +1029, Tests +1407, Docs +34. Total +2470 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: Partially: current-main source shows the same-chat WhatsApp action path requires an explicit target and the QA driver derives group sender phone from the group JID. The CI lint failure is proven from GitHub logs; I did not run a live WhatsApp device lane.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The patch-local lint failures are concrete and mechanical enough for an automated repair attempt; live WhatsApp proof and maintainer review still remain after that repair.

target.title: test(qa-lab): harden whatsapp qa scenarios
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The WhatsApp QA lane was missing coverage for several visible plugin behaviors, and a few existing scenarios were not proving the exact behavior users see in WhatsApp.

The clearest example was quoted replies: the old quote-reply scenario could pass without verifying that the outgoing WhatsApp message actually quoted the correct inbound message. That left room for the reply quote feature itself to regress while QA still passed.

This PR hardens the WhatsApp QA lane by adding more real WhatsApp scenarios, tightening existing ones, and adding the small runtime/test support needed for those scenarios to prove the right behavior.

## Why This Change Was Made

### New WhatsApp QA scenarios

This adds coverage for user-visible WhatsApp behaviors that could previously regress without a focused scenario catching them:

- `whatsapp-agent-message-action-react`: sends a normal WhatsApp DM, lets the agent use the `message` tool, and verifies the native WhatsApp emoji reaction appears on the trigger message.
- `whatsapp-agent-message-action-upload-file`: drives an agent `message(action=upload-file)` path and verifies visible WhatsApp media is sent.
- `whatsapp-reply-to-mode-batched`: sends two messages close together and verifies the agent can see the earlier queued message in the same run context.
- `whatsapp-status-reaction-lifecycle`: verifies the visible status-reaction sequence around ack/reply cleanup, including order.
- `whatsapp-group-pending-history-context`: protects group pending-history context so queued group messages remain available to the agent.
- `whatsapp-broadcast-group-fanout`: verifies one mentioned group message can produce distinct visible replies from two configured agents.
- `whatsapp-group-activation-always`: changes a group to `/activation always`, verifies an unmentioned group message wakes the agent, then restores `/activation mention`.
- `whatsapp-group-reply-to-bot-triggers`: seeds a bot reply and verifies a native quoted reply to that bot message can wake the agent without an explicit mention.
- `whatsapp-inbound-reaction-no-trigger`: verifies an inbound user reaction is observed as a reaction event and does not start a fresh agent run.

### Existing scenario improvements

This also tightens scenarios that already existed but were not strict enough:

- Reply/quote delivery now verifies the actual quoted WhatsApp message id, including long final replies that are split into multiple chunks.
- Batched reply-to-mode can no longer pass as a normal second-message reply; the mock provider only returns the success marker when both messages are present in the same request context.
- Status reaction matching now enforces observed event order instead of accepting the right events in the wrong sequence.
- Group activation restore now runs even if the visible probe fails, so the QA lane does not leave the test group in `/activation always`.
- Broadcast QA now stages mock auth for all broadcast agents, not only the default QA agents.
- WhatsApp evidence now records each scenario posture (`user-path`, `direct-gateway`, or `native-approval`) so scenario results are easier to interpret.

### WhatsApp plugin support fixes

The scenario work exposed a couple of plugin-side gaps that needed to be fixed for the QA lane to test real behavior:

- The WhatsApp QA driver now keeps group identity and participant identity separate. Group messages keep `fromJid` as the group JID while exposing `participantJid` and participant phone metadata separately.
- WhatsApp same-chat `message` actions can infer the current chat from tool context for reactions and file uploads while still using the existing target authorization path.

## User Impact

Users should not see a new command or configuration surface from this PR. The impact is better regression protection for WhatsApp behavior that users do see: quote replies, reactions, media sends, group activation, group reply context, broadcast fanout, status reactions, and inbound reaction handling.

For maintainers, WhatsApp QA should now give a much clearer signal when changes affect visible WhatsApp behavior instead of only proving low-level Gateway calls.

## Evidence

Local verification:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/qa-driver.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-react-action.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`

Review/proof:

- Full `$master-quality-gate` run with subagents.
- Final autoreview was clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Final LOC pass was clean with no required shrink/bloat findings.

Known gap:

- I did not run a live WhatsApp/Baileys device lane for this branch. The proof here is source, unit, type, docs, and review proof.
target.author: mcaxtr

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
